title: AI can interact with people appropriately and work with them
description: An AI system can model people well enough to communicate clearly, adapt to their context, and collaborate without creating avoidable confusion or friction.

subQuestions.title: Can it model user goals and knowledge level?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system infers what the user is trying to do and how much context or explanation they need.
subQuestions.rationale: User modeling is already strong in many bounded assistant settings, especially where interaction history is short and goals are practical.

proofItems.title: Controlled study: User-goal and knowledge-level inference
proofItems.shortExplanation: Controlled interaction studies show strong performance in identifying user intent and choosing an appropriate explanation depth.

subQuestions.title: Can it adapt tone and explanation to its audience?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system can shift style, depth, and framing without losing task correctness.
subQuestions.rationale: Tone adaptation is one of the most mature and consistently high-rated aspects of current conversational models.

proofItems.title: Expert blind review: Audience-adapted tone and explanation
proofItems.source: Expert Review Panel on AI Performance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Blinded reviewers consistently rate top systems highly on style adaptation across novice, expert, and stressed-user scenarios.

subQuestions.title: Can it recognize confusion, frustration, or disagreement?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system can detect negative conversational states before the interaction breaks down.
subQuestions.rationale: Detection is increasingly useful on explicit cues, but quieter forms of frustration and disagreement remain easy to miss.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Detection of confusion and frustration in live chats
proofItems.shortExplanation: Conversation audits show improving recognition of explicit frustration, but still document late recognition of subtle confusion and disagreement.

subQuestions.title: Can it handle collaboration and handoff well?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system can maintain shared context, divide work, and hand off cleanly to people when needed.
subQuestions.rationale: Collaborative behavior is promising, but real-world handoffs still fail when context is implicit or the workflow spans multiple people and tools.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Collaboration and handoff quality
proofItems.shortExplanation: Operational evaluations show better collaboration structure and note quality, but still expose weak handoffs across larger multi-person workflows.

subQuestions.title: Can it interpret nuance, implied meaning, and differing perspectives?
subQuestions.description: Measures pragmatic understanding beyond literal surface meaning in social and cooperative settings.
subQuestions.rationale: Nuance handling is clearly better than before, but still inconsistent in culturally loaded, indirect, or high-context exchanges.

proofItems.title: Expert blind review: Nuance and perspective interpretation
proofItems.source: Expert Review Panel on AI Performance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Expert interaction panels found many strong responses, but still documented noticeable gaps on indirect meaning and subtle perspective shifts.
